justification: All pass criteria met: Both queues (order.fulfillment, order.fulfillment.dlq) identified by name in integration table and infrastructure table. Both env vars (RABBITMQ_URL, FULFILLMENT_DLQ_NAME) identified by name. Escalation routed to Coach K (all four escalations go through Coach K). new_infrastructure_required section populated with both queues and env vars in a structured table. Verdict is NOT READY. Observability correctly noted as adequate in isolation — logging is 'Adequate', health endpoint 'Correct', metrics present — with the infrastructure gap as the primary blocker.

observations.text: Thorough infrastructure gap identification — went beyond the rubric minimum by also flagging exchange configuration, DLQ routing rules, and questioning whether RabbitMQ even exists in the stack
new_infrastructure_required section is well-structured with categories, items, and status columns covering env vars, queues, services, and config changes
Correctly distinguishes that observability basics (logging, health, metrics) are present and adequate — the blocker is infrastructure, not observability
Escalations all route through Coach K with secondary routing suggestions (MJ for architecture, Bird for domain, Shaq for implementation), which is correct protocol

Escalation routing says 'Coach K → MJ (architecture review) + Infra Team' rather than a clean escalation to Coach K alone — this partially dilutes the escalation by pre-deciding Coach K's routing, though Coach K is still the primary recipient
Goes well beyond minimum requirements with resilience assessment, rollback planning, and concrete must-have recommendations
Identifies additional real risks not in the rubric (idempotency, poison messages, reconnection strategy) showing genuine operational thinking
